data.title: Honeywell Stock Falls After Earnings Beat. Here's Why. — Barrons.com
data.description:
Al Root

Industrial conglomerate Honeywell delivered a solid beat-and-raise quarter as it prepares to break into three pieces.

Honeywell reported adjusted earnings per share of $2.75 from sales of $10.4 billion on Thursday morning. EPS exceeded the top end of the company's guidance range of $2.70.

Wall Street was looking for EPS of $2.66 from sales of $10.1 billion, according to FactSet. A year ago, in the second quarter of 2024, Honeywell reported EPS of $2.49 from sales of $9.6 billion.

"Honeywell delivered outstanding results in the second quarter with both organic growth and adjusted earnings per share exceeding guidance despite the unpredictable macroeconomic backdrop," said CEO Vimal Kapur in a news release. "With Building Automation leading the way, three out of four segments grew sales at better than 5% in the quarter."

Building sales grew 8% year over year. Aerospace and energy-related sales were up 6%. Industrial automation sales were flat, with demand in Europe "challenged."

Overall, quarterly results look strong. Guidance is ahead of the Street as well. For the full year, Honeywell expects earnings per share to land between, $10.45 and $10.65. The $10.55 midpoint is up 20 cents from prior guidance. Wall Street currently projects EPS of $10.42. The new midpoint implies second-half EPS of about $5.29 per share, a hair better than the $5.26 that analysts currently project.

Results come as the company prepares to break apart. In February, Honeywell laid out plans to break apart into three companies — one dedicated to automation, one to aerospace, and another to advanced materials.

The breakup is designed to help unlock value. Honeywell shares trade for about 21 times estimated 2026 earnings. GE Aerospace trades for 39 times estimated 2026 earnings. Rockwell Automation trades for about 31 times.

Making some assumptions about how each part would trade, Honeywell, broken apart, could trade for the equivalent of 30 times 2026 earnings, or about $330 a share. That's only rough math to illustrate one reason why investors are optimistic about a Honeywell split.

The materials spin off should be completed by late 2025. The automation and aerospace separation is slated for late 2026.

Coming into Thursday trading, Honeywell stock was up about 6% year to date, trailing the S&P 500 by about two percentage points. Shares, however, have been strong recently, up about 21% over the past three months.

Write to Al Root at allen.root@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Meme stocks mixed a day after retail trader-infused run
data.description:
** Shares of meme stocks are mixed after a retail trader-driven frenzy in the last session

** Residential real estate platform Opendoor Technologies jumps 14.5% to $2.6

** OPEN has seen heavy retail interest following bullish posts from Canadian hedge fund manager Eric Jackson on X.com last week

** Krispy Kreme up 2.8%; stock had surged as much as 38% intraday on Wednesday, closing about 4% higher

** Mom-and-pop traders bought $3.9 mln worth of DNUT shares on Wednesday; highest since May 2024, according to Vanda Research data

** Action camera maker GoPro (GRPO.O) down 6.5% after spiking more than 12% in the last session

** Retail buying for GRPO shares was the highest on Wednesday since March 2021

** U.S. department store company Kohl's , which jumped on a retail trader-driven move earlier this week, last up 1.7%

** Plant-based meat company Beyond Meat down 3.8%, online gifts retailer 1-800-Flowers.com off 3.2%

** Some stocks were trending on retail investor-focused social media platform Stocktwits and Reddit on Wednesday

** Most stocks also have relatively high short interest, according to LSEG data

data.title: S&P 500, Nasdaq up early as Alphabet offsets Tesla drop
data.description:
Key points:
  • S&P 500, Nasdaq slightly green; Dow dips
  • Jun new home sales 0.627M vs 0.650M estimate
  • Euro STOXX 600 index up ~0.3%; ECB leaves rates unchanged
  • Dollar ~flat; bitcoin up; crude gains >1%; gold dips
  • U.S. 10-Year Treasury yield edges up to ~4.41%

S&P 500, NASDAQ UP EARLY AS ALPHABET OFFSETS TESLA DROP

The S&P 500 and Nasdaq are up in early trading on Thursday as shares of Alphabet gained 2.5% after the company late Wednesday hiked its capital spending plans for the year and cited demand for its cloud computing services.

On the flip side, shares of Tesla are falling sharply, a day after Chief Executive Elon Musk said U.S. government cuts in support for electric vehicle makers could lead to a "few rough quarters" for the company.

Tesla shares are down more than 8% in early trading.

The Dow is lower, with IBM down about 10% after its second-quarter earnings disappointed. Shares of Honeywell also weighed on the Dow following its quarterly results.

Here is the morning market snapshot from just shortly after 10:00 a.m. EDT:

data.title: ICE Canada Morning Comment: Canola Slipping Back
data.description:
WINNIPEG, Manitoba--Intercontinental Exchange canola futures slipped back on Thursday morning, unable to retain overnight increases.

The Prairies continue to expect rain over the weekend. Those areas that recently received precipitation are likely see their crops maintained. However, crops in dry areas will need rain very soon.

Support for canola came from gains in the Chicago soy complex, while European rapeseed was mixed and Malaysian palm oil was down slightly. Upticks in crude oil underpinned the vegetable oils.

The November canola contract edged above its 20- and 50-day moving averages, which were 50 cents apart. The November remained handily above its 100- and 200-day moving averages.

Manitoba Agriculture reported the province's canola ranged from fully podded in the earliest planted fields to early bolting for fields seeded later.

The Canadian dollar slipped back on Thursday morning, with the loonie at 73.39 U.S. cents compared to Wednesday's close of 73.48.

Approximately 8,150 contracts were traded by 9:41 EDT and prices in Canadian dollars per metric tonne were:

data.title: Ceconomy Shares Jump on Potential $2.6 Billion Takeover by JD.com
data.description:
By Andrea Figueras

Shares in Ceconomy surged after the electronics retailer said it is in advanced talks with China's JD.com over a potential takeover offer that would value the German group at around $2.6 billion.

The Beijing-based online retail company is considering a cash offer of 4.60 euros for each ordinary share, Ceconomy said. The bid would value the target company at 2.23 billion euros ($2.63 billion).

In European afternoon trading, the German company's stock surged more than 14% to 4.29 euros, Over the year to date, shares have risen by roughly 61%.

The companies haven't yet signed any legally binding agreements, meaning it isn't certain whether a takeover offer will actually be made or not, Ceconomy said.

A representative for JD.com declined to comment.

Write to Andrea Figueras at andrea.figueras@wsj.com

data.title: Alphabet Is Maintained at Buy by Roth Capital
US New Home Sales Miss Expectations
data.description:
Sales of new single-family homes in the United States rose 0.6% in June 2025 to a seasonally adjusted annualized rate of 627,000 units, slightly up from May’s seven-month low of 623,000 but still well below market expectations of 650,000.

The data indicate continued pressure on the housing market, as high mortgage rates and economic uncertainty have led many buyers to delay purchasing decisions.

Regionally, new home sales increased in the South (up 5.1% to 390,000) and the Midwest (up 6.3% to 85,000), while declining sharply in the Northeast (down 27.6% to 21,000) and the West (down 8.4% to 131,000).

data.title: Thomasville Community Development Corporation Helps Tackle Senior Health With Plant-Based Diet Options
data.description:
NORTHAMPTON, MA / ACCESS Newswire / July 24, 2025 / Ray C. Anderson Foundation:

By: Layan Abu Tarboush - WTXL-TV

A new plant-based food program is helping Thomasville, Georgia seniors manage chronic illnesses through free meals, health screenings, and community support.

Fifty seniors are served weekly with four free plant-based meals. Health data is tracked through A1C, glucose, and BMI testing, and the program powered locally by local farms, chefs, and labs.

The program is one of several new programs that Thomasville Community Development Corporation is able to implement as recipients of a two-year grant ($100,000 this year) from Drawdown Georgia.

Watch the video here.

Photo Credit: Layan Abu Tarboush - WTXL-TV

data.title: Union Pacific, Norfolk Southern in Advanced Discussions for Deal — WSJ
data.description:
By Connor Hart

Union Pacific and Norfolk Southern said they are in advanced discussions about a potential combination, which would create the largest rail operator in the country.

"There are no assurances that we'll reach an agreement, but we are talking," Union Pacific Chief Executive Jim Vena said on a call with analysts Thursday.

The disclosure confirms a Wall Street Journal report.

A deal could create a sprawling rail network that spans the continent and handles a sizable share of freight across the U.S. Currently, no railroad operator has a network that runs coast to coast in the U.S.

Vena has previously spoken publicly about the benefits of a transcontinental railroad, stating that it would improve service by smoothing out current delays at interchanges, where railroad operators transfer railcars to another operator.

Still, any deal would face serious scrutiny from a series of regulators, including the Surface Transportation Board, the economic regulator primarily overseeing freight railroads, as well as the Justice Department, investors, Amtrak and labor unions.

The last time federal regulators approved a major railroad merger was in 2023. Canadian Pacific Railway and Kansas City Southern sought to merge in a deal to create the first freight rail network linking Canada, the U.S. and Mexico.

Union Pacific and Norfolk Southern said they don't intend to make additional comments on the matter unless required or otherwise appropriate.

Separately, Union Pacific logged slightly higher profit and revenue in the second quarter and reaffirmed its full-year outlook, despite also warning of potential challenges in the second half of the year.

If completed, a deal could mark the largest corporate transaction this year, the Journal said. Union Pacific has a market value of around $140 billion, while Norfolk is valued at about $60 billion.

Shares of Union Pacific were trading 1.8% lower at $226.43 in early trading Thursday. Shares of Norfolk Southern climbed 1.3% to $283.85.

Write to Connor Hart at Connor.Hart@wsj.com

MORE U.S. JUNE NEW HOME SALES: MEDIAN SALES PRICE -2.0% NSA TO $501,000 (MAY REVISED TO $511,500 FROM $522,200 ORIGINALLY REPORTED)
American Airlines Restores Profit Outlook With Lower Target — Update
data.description:
By Dean Seal

American Airlines Group reissued profit guidance for the year with a lower target than it had in January, before tariffs and a domestic travel slowdown prompted major airlines to withdraw their outlooks.

The Fort Worth, Texas-based carrier on Thursday said its bottom line for 2025, on an adjusted basis, is expected to come in between a 20-cent loss and an 80-cent profit.

The company forecast full-year adjusted earnings of $1.70 to $2.70 a share in January, days before a mid-air plane collision over the Potomac River in Washington, D.C., which airlines later cited as putting pressure on air-travel demand. Then in April, American and other U.S. airlines withdrew full-year guidance after swirling tariffs clouded their financial-forecasting abilities.

American said Thursday that the high end of its new outlook should be achievable if domestic demand gets stronger, while the low end would come into view if new macroeconomic weakness emerges.

"Let's face it, the domestic network has been under stress because of the uncertainty in the economy and the reluctance of domestic passengers to get in the game," Chief Executive Robert Isom said on a call with analysts.

Shares fell 7.8% to $11.70 in early trading.

For the current quarter that started this month, American expects to log an adjusted loss of 10 cents to 60 cents a share. Analysts polled by FactSet had been projecting a small 3-cent per-share adjusted profit.

Executives said third-quarter revenue could fall as much as 2% or rise up to 1%. July will likely be one of the weakest months of the year, Chief Financial Officer Devon May said. American and the rest of the industry ratcheted up capacity during the month, and July travel had been largely booked out early, before demand trends started improving in recent weeks.

"We believe the worst is behind us, and year-over-year revenue will sequentially improve each month," May said.

For the second quarter that wrapped up last month, American posted a profit of $599 million, or 91 cents a share, compared with $717 million, or $1.01 a share, in the same quarter a year earlier.

Stripping out one-time items, adjusted earnings were 95 cents a share. Analysts polled by FactSet had been projecting 78 cents a share.

Revenue rose about half a percentage point to $14.4 billion, above analyst projections for $14.29 billion, according to FactSet.

The company said revenue from domestic passengers was down 2% while capacity was up almost 5%. That weakness in domestic leisure offset higher demand for premium seating and international flights. "Premium demand and spending from higher-income consumers remain resilient," Isom said.

Analysts had predicted that airlines' second quarter wouldn't be as bad as many had feared, with declining earnings but some slight revenue growth on higher capacity as demand stabilized.

Delta Air Lines restored its full-year guidance earlier this month and said second-quarter revenue was flat but ahead of Wall Street estimates, sending its shares higher. The airline noted that some weaker travel trends had persisted into the latest completed quarter, but projected stronger third-quarter earnings than what analysts had anticipated.

United Airlines followed a week later with a report that surging international travel had tripled its second-quarter profit, prompting an outlook raise. U.S. travelers have been cutting back on their domestic flying, which had been particularly popular immediately following the pandemic, and have opted recently for more global trips.

Write to Dean at dean.seal@wsj.com

data.title: Blackstone rises after Q2 profit beat
data.description:
** Shares of alternative asset manager Blackstone rise 2.5% to $176.38 in early trade

** Co beats Q2 profit expectations on strong gains in its credit and private equity businesses and a pickup in performance-related fees tied to perpetual capital funds

** Asset sales in the credit and insurance segment were $10 billion, while the company also sold $7.3 billion of private equity assets. It had $181.2 billion of capital available for deployment

** Distributable earnings, which represent cash that can be used to pay dividends, grew 25% to $1.6 billion, or $1.21 per share, for the three months ended June 30 compared to analysts' expectation of $1.10 apiece

** Inflows of $52.1 billion helped push Blackstone's total AUM to $1.2 trillion, up 13% from a year ago

** As of last close, BX share were down marginally in 2025

data.title: WESTINGHOUSE AIR BRAKE TECHNOLOGIES CORP SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Westinghouse Air Brake Technologies Corp (Wabtec), a leading provider of equipment, systems, digital solutions, and value-added services for the freight and transit rail sectors, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report highlights the company's financial and operational performance, showcasing growth in key areas despite challenging market conditions.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Net Sales: $2,706 million, an increase of $62 million or 2.3% compared to the same period in 2024, driven by higher demand in the Transit Segment.
  • Gross Profit: $938 million, up from $874 million in the prior year, indicating improved profitability.
  • Income from Operations: $472 million, an increase from $430 million in the second quarter of 2024, showcasing effective cost management.
  • Net Income Attributable to Wabtec Shareholders: $336 million, compared to $289 million in the previous year, highlighting strong financial performance.
  • Diluted EPS: $1.96, up from $1.64 in the second quarter of 2024, reflecting increased earnings per share.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The Freight Segment reported sales of $1,919 million, with Services contributing $781 million, Equipment $546 million, Components $401 million, and Digital Intelligence $191 million. The Transit Segment reported sales of $787 million, with Original Equipment Manufacturer sales at $353 million and Aftermarket sales at $434 million.
  • Geographical Performance: Approximately half of the company's net sales in the first six months of 2025 came from customers outside the United States, indicating a strong international presence.
  • Sales Units: The Freight Segment experienced a decrease in Equipment sales due to lower mining sales and reduced locomotive deliveries, while the Transit Segment saw increased demand for Original Equipment Manufacturing and Aftermarket products driven by investments in sustainable infrastructure and increased passenger ridership.
  • Future Outlook: The company is focused on driving operational efficiency and improving profitability through restructuring initiatives such as Integration 3.0, Portfolio Optimization, and Integration 2.0. These initiatives aim to consolidate the footprint, reduce complexity, and streamline operations. Additionally, the company is mitigating macroeconomic volatility through price escalations, cost mitigation efforts, and strategic sourcing.

SEC Filing: WESTINGHOUSE AIR BRAKE TECHNOLOGIES CORP [ WAB ] - 10-Q - Jul. 24, 2025

data.title: Nasdaq jumps on market-beating results, bright IPO market outlook
data.description:
** Shares of exchange operator Nasdaq rise ~6.3% to $93.86 in morning trading

** NDAQ profit beats estimates on strong demand for its financial technology products and a surge in trading activity

** Co posts Q2 adjusted profit of 85 cents per share vs estimates of 81 cents, according to data compiled by LSEG

** Revenue of $1.31 billion in the quarter also tops Wall Street expectations of $1.28 billion

** "Strong performance of recent listings, especially of large-cap companies, has raised optimism for the IPO outlook for the remainder of this year and into 2026," CEO Adena Friedman says

** Stock up 21% YTD, including session moves

data.title: New York's electric grid prepared to meet hot weather demand, operator says
data.description:
The New York Independent System Operator (NYISO) said on Thursday that it is prepared to meet power demand on Friday as a heat dome moving across the Southern states is expected to increase temperatures in its footprint.

The forecasted baseline peak demand for Friday is 29,000 megawatts (MW), NYISO said in a statement.

NYISO’s latest summer readiness assessment reports 40,937 MW of available power resources statewide, with 3,159 MW accessible through emergency procedures to maintain grid reliability if needed.

New York recorded a record peak of 33,956 MW at the end of a week-long heat wave in July 2013, the operator added.

data.title: Securities Fraud Class Action Filed Against Reddit, Inc. (RDDT) - Levi & Korsinsky Reminds Investors of August 18, 2025
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / July 24, 2025 / If you suffered a loss on your Reddit, Inc. investment and want to learn about a potential recovery under the federal securities laws, follow the link below for more information:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/reddit-inc-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=157976&wire=1&utm_campaign=27

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq. via email at jlevi@levikorsinsky.com or call (212) 363-7500 to speak to our team of experienced shareholder advocates.

THE LAWSUIT: A class action securities lawsuit was filed against Reddit, Inc. that seeks to recover losses of shareholders who were adversely affected by alleged securities fraud between October 29, 2024 and May 20, 2025.

CASE DETAILS: The filed complaint alleges that defendants made false statements and/or concealed that: (i) changes in Google Search's algorithm and features like AI Overview were causing users to stop their query on Google Search; (ii) these algorithm changes were materially different than prior instances of reduced traffic to the Reddit website; (iii) defendants were aware that the increase in the query term "Reddit" on search engines was because users were getting the sought after answer from Google Search without having to go to Reddit, and not because they intended to visit Reddit; (iv) this zero-click search reality was dramatically reducing traffic to Reddit in a manner the Company was unable to overcome in the short term; (v) defendants, therefore, lacked a reasonable basis for their outlook on user rates and advertising revenues; and (vi) as a result, the Company's public statements were materially false and misleading at all relevant times.

WHAT'S NEXT? If you suffered a loss in Reddit, Inc. stock during the relevant time frame - even if you still hold your shares - go to https://zlk.com/pslra-1/reddit-inc-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=157976&wire=1&utm_campaign=27 to learn about your rights to seek a recovery. There is no cost or obligation to participate.

WHY LEVI & KORSINSKY: Over the past 20 years, Levi & Korsinsky LLP has established itself as a nationally-recognized securities litigation firm that has secured hundreds of millions of dollars for aggrieved shareholders and built a track record of winning high-stakes cases. The firm has extensive expertise representing investors in complex securities litigation and a team of over 70 employees to serve our clients. For seven years in a row, Levi & Korsinsky has ranked in ISS Securities Class Action Services' Top 50 Report as one of the top securities litigation firms in the United States. Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.

CONTACT:

Levi & Korsinsky, LLP

Joseph E. Levi, Esq.

Ed Korsinsky, Esq.

33 Whitehall Street, 17th Floor

New York, NY 10004

jlevi@levikorsinsky.com

Tel: (212) 363-7500

Fax: (212) 363-7171

https://zlk.com/

data.title: L3Harris Stock Rises on Earnings Beat. This Is a 'Clear Inflection Point.' — Barrons.com
data.description:
Al Root

L3Harris Technologies reported better-than-expected second-quarter results. The stock is up with the company at a "clear inflection point."

Thursday morning, the defense contractor announced second-quarter earnings per share of $2.78 from sales of $5.4 billion. Wall Street was looking for earnings per share of $2.48 from sales of $5.3 billion, according to FactSet. A year ago, the company reported EPS of $3.24 from sales of $5.2 billion.

New orders in the quarter were strong at $8.3 billion, 1.5 times sales.

Financial guidance moved higher as well. Management expects EPS to land between $10.40 and $10.60 a share. The midpoint of $10.50 is up a dime from prior guidance. Wall Street currently projects $10.49 a share, according to FactSet.

L3Harris stock was up 2% at $275 in early trading, while S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average futures were up 0.1% and down 0.4%, respectively.

"This marks a clear inflection point, with our strongest top-line growth in six quarters and meaningful progress towards our 2026 Financial Framework," said CEO Christopher Kubasik in a news release.

The 2026 framework, revealed in late 2023 aimed for 2026 sales of $23 billion and operating profit margins of 16%. Wall Street currently projects 2026 sales of just under $23 billion and operating profit margins just north of 16%.

"Defense is entering a generational investment cycle, as U.S. and allied budgets grow rapidly," added Kubasik. "Demand is accelerating, and our portfolio is aligned with key growth areas — Golden Dome, space, missiles, shipbuilding, autonomy, and resilient communications."

President Donald Trump announced his Golden Dome missile-defense shield for the U.S. in May. It will cost hundreds of billions to develop, according to government estimates.

Overall, it's a solid report. More importantly, management feels confident about the future. Investors feel good too. Coming into Thursday trading, L3Harris stock was up about 28% this year.

Write to Al Root at allen.root@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / July 24, 2025 / Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC is investigating potential claims on behalf of purchasers of DMC Global Inc. ("DMC Global" or "the Company") . Investors who purchased DMC Global securities prior to January 29, 2024, and continue to hold to the present, are encouraged to obtain additional information and assist the investigation by visiting the firm's site: bgandg.com/BOOM.

data.title: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC Encourages Mereo BioPharma Group plc (MREO) Stockholders to Inquire about Securities Investigation
data.description:
NEW YORK CITY, NY / ACCESS Newswire / July 24, 2025 / Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC is investigating potential claims on behalf of purchasers of Mereo BioPharma Group plc ("Mereo" or "the Company") . Investors who purchased Mereo securities are encouraged to obtain additional information and assist the investigation by visiting the firm's site: bgandg.com/MREO.

Investigation Details

On July 9, 2025, Mereo and its development partner Ultragenyx Pharmaceutical Inc. issued a press release "announc[ing] that the randomized, placebo-controlled Phase 3 portion of the Orbit study evaluating UX143 (setrusumab) in pediatric and young adult patients with osteogenesis imperfecta (OI) is progressing toward a final analysis[.]" Following a Data Monitoring Committee meeting, the two companies advised that the final analysis would occur "around the end of the year." Following this news, Mereo's American Depositary Receipt ("ADR") price fell $1.25 per share, or 42.52%, to close at $1.69 per share on July 10, 2025.
